compiled_state.title: Non-blocking test failure (skill eval) written as 'failed' in test-gate-status — blocks commit despite being non-blocking
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## What happened

record-test-status.sh correctly identifies a skill eval failure as non-blocking ('WARNING: Skill eval failed (exit 1) — non-blocking at commit time. Daily CI will catch regressions.'). However, it writes 'failed' to the test-gate-status file. The pre-commit test gate reads this file and blocks the commit.

## Expected behavior

Non-blocking failures should NOT cause the test-gate-status file to be written as 'failed'. Either:
1. The status file should distinguish between blocking and non-blocking failures
2. Non-blocking failures should be recorded as warnings but the status should remain 'passed'
3. The pre-commit hook should read a more nuanced status that includes a blocking/non-blocking flag

## Security constraint

The fix MUST NOT allow agents to bypass the test gate for commits that fail required tests. The distinction is between:
- Required tests (unit tests, integration tests) — these MUST block commit
- Non-blocking checks (skill evals, daily CI checks) — these should warn but not block

## Fix guidance

Consider updating the messages and status format rather than changing which tests block. The agent needs clear guidance about what 'failed' means in context — if the status file says 'failed' but the failure is non-blocking, the agent should see a message like 'test-gate-status: passed (with non-blocking warnings: skill eval)' rather than 'test-gate-status: failed'.

comments.body: INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED: safeguard_approval | Behavioral code bug: non-blocking test failure blocks commit. Fix requires editing plugins/dso/scripts/record-test-status.sh (safeguard). Re-run in interactive session to approve.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 2 (BASIC). Cluster with e10a-a63e (same file: record-test-status.sh)
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Investigation RESULT: ROOT_CAUSE: Possible misdiagnosis. Code correctly treats skill eval failures as non-blocking (STATUS not modified). Real issue may be that skill files fuzzy-match to test files in tests/ which fail, and that unit test failure sets STATUS=failed. CONFIDENCE: medium. FIX: Clarify whether skill-only commits should bypass fuzzy test matching, or if the warning message is the real issue.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Investigation conclusion: Code is correct. Skill eval failures do NOT set STATUS to 'failed' (verified — lines 718-728 only emit WARNING, STATUS is never modified). The commit block the reporter observed was caused by unit test failures (STATUS set on lines 578-685), not skill eval failures. The WARNING message about skill eval failure coincided with the unit test failure, creating an attribution error. Recommend closing as 'working as designed' or improving the STATUS output message to include the cause.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
